DDR5-6400.reason: NEMIX $1,488 Day 29 anchor (8th week). DDR5 spot $41.667 three-day ATH plateau — sellers refusing to lower. 32 days full-chain sold out. Samsung warm-down production cut 360K wafers in effect. DDR5 profitability surpasses HBM3e → capacity reallocation positive for DDR5 supply. No reason to wait.

DDR5-5600.reason: Spot $41.667 three-day ATH plateau. 96GB NEMIX $3,280 Day 28 stable anchor (7th formal tracking column request). Micron CEO '2027+' tight supply. DDR5 capacity reallocation signal positive. No reason to wait.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: NAND Q1 revised up to +85-90% (was +70-75%). Q2 +70-75%. Brand split 2.5-2.7x structural Day 24. Goldman Sachs 4.2% NAND deficit Day 7. 5-year SSD LTA becoming standard — large customers locking supply, small buyers squeezed. 32 days full-chain sold out. Tracking lag 27-155% does not reduce procurement urgency.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: SS 9550 Pro $17,700 Day 21 FIVE-WEEK MILESTONE — longest sustained price increase in tracking history. CDW-G In Stock provides viable procurement path but wholesale (pack-of-10) still backordered. SHI $19,573 backorder. PM9D3a 30.72TB not deliverable Day 10. Samsung HBM packaging slowdown adds upstream risk to PCIe5 supply chain. 6-reference 5-source strongest anchor cluster maintained.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Mouser 61.44TB U.2 Day 9 still only 1 unit. BUT Connection $33,048/GovConnection $33,213 In Stock + CDW 8-10 day ETA provide viable alternative channels. Crisis downgraded from 'total unavailability' to 'channel fragmentation with 34% premium.' 2026 Q3/Q4 QLC procurement: must lock through Connection/CDW now. 2027 Q1 procurement: may wait for Sept 7 replenishment but no guarantee. 831% full-series price surge + Goldman Sachs 4.2% NAND deficit + 5-year LTA trend support urgency.

gaps.note: SEVENTH formal request. NEMIX $3,280 Day 28 continuous anchor. HPE $3,665 / Dell $4,100 / Hynix OEM $3,020-$3,650 cross-validation exceeding 4 weeks. Sample quality exceeds multiple existing tracking columns.

VDURA $17,500 benchmark + multi-source validation exists but DiscTech offline means real-time price not incorporated into tracking.

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung bonus resentment → HBM packaging intentional production slowdowns 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. More insidious than strike — no clear endpoint.
escalatedRisks.source: Tom's Hardware / Strait Times / StartupFortune / Investing.com May 27-28 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Newly ratified agreement creates DS (semiconductor) $400K vs DX (consumer) $4K bonus disparity (100:1). Resentment has spread to TSP (Test & Package) divisions with reports of 'work negligence,' 'canceled meetings,' and 'intentional production slowdowns.' HBM delivery schedules impacted. Packaging capacity shared with enterprise SSD packaging — indirect risk. Unlike strike (can be ended by agreement), organizational resentment has no clear resolution mechanism.
escalatedRisks.confidence: medium-high (multi-source qualitative, no quantitative impact data yet)

escalatedRisks.risk: DDR5 spot Phase 5 high-level consolidation — Day 3 ATH plateau ⚡
escalatedRisks.status: ONGOING — Day 3. Third consecutive day at $41.667 (0.00%). Directional inflection window approaching.
escalatedRisks.detail: After 5 consecutive daily gains (+$0.90, +2.21%), DDR5 spot has now plateaued at $41.667 for THREE consecutive sessions. TrendForce: 'gains narrowing amid sluggish trading, most inquiries focused on price trends rather than purchases.' Classic 'high-level digestion' pattern: buyers sitting on sidelines, sellers refusing to lower. If breaks above $41.667 → Phase 6 (new leg up); if drops below $41.00 → short-term correction signal.

escalatedRisks.risk: Mouser QLC 61.44TB U.2 structural supply crisis ⚡⚡⚡ — Day 9 but alternative channels confirmed
escalatedRisks.status: ONGOING — Day 9. Crisis partially mitigated by alternative channel discovery.
escalatedRisks.detail: Mouser U.2 61.44TB only 1 unit for 9th consecutive day. 4 on order Sept 7 (3.4 months). BUT Connection $33,048 In Stock 1-2 day ship + GovConnection $33,213 In Stock 24-48hr + CDW 8-10 day ETA provide viable alternative procurement channels. 34% channel premium vs Newegg $24,600 reflects supply tightness not total unavailability. Crisis downgraded from 'total channel closure' to 'high-premium channel fragmentation.'

escalatedRisks.risk: NAND Q1 contract revised UP to +85-90% (was +70-75%) 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1 upward revision from TrendForce 5/25.
escalatedRisks.detail: NAND flash contract prices climbed +85-90% QoQ in Q1 2026, exceeding previous estimates of +70-75%. Top 5 NAND suppliers combined revenue +83.7% QoQ for Q1. This means upstream NAND pricing was MORE aggressive than previously modeled. Q2 +70-75% still projected. 5-year SSD/HDD Long-Term Agreements becoming industry standard. Enterprise SSD +60%. Implies PCIe4 TLC tracking lag may be worse than current 27-155% estimate.

escalatedRisks.risk: DDR5 profitability surpassing HBM3e → capacity reallocation 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. Medium-term positive for DDR5 supply, negative for HBM pricing.
escalatedRisks.detail: TrendForce projects DDR5 profitability will exceed HBM3e starting Q1 2026. HBM3e/DDR5 ASP gap narrowing from 4-5x to 1-2x by end-2026. Suppliers may reallocate production capacity from HBM3e to server DDR5. This is structurally positive for DDR5 supply availability in H2 2026-2027 but creates upward pricing pressure on HBM.
escalatedRisks.confidence: medium (TrendForce projection, actual reallocation pace unknown)

escalatedRisks.risk: SS Micron 9550 Pro 30.72TB five-week milestone — Day 21 ⚡
escalatedRisks.status: MILESTONE — Day 21. Longest sustained price increase in tracking history.
escalatedRisks.source: ServerSupply + SHI + CDW-G + Xi + Tech-America + Newegg
escalatedRisks.detail: SS 9550 Pro $17,700 has sustained for 21 consecutive days (from $16,600 on 5/8, +$1,100, +6.6%). This is the longest sustained pricing increase in tracking history. CDW-G In Stock $17,274.70 provides viable procurement path but wholesale channels remain tight. SHI $19,573 still backordered. Micron 5-ref cluster median $17,318 stable. Samsung PM9D3a 30.72TB bottleneck Day 10.

escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. Structural supply constraint signal.
escalatedRisks.detail: Crushing shortages have pushed customers to sign record 5-year Long-Term Agreements for SSDs and HDDs. Large hyperscalers and cloud providers locking in supply through 2030-2031. Implication: small/medium buyers who don't sign LTAs will face increasing difficulty accessing supply. This is a structural market change — not a temporary squeeze.

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung union vote ratified — strike risk CLOSED 🔴
escalatedRisks.source: Korea JoongAng Daily / Korea Herald / Chosun Ilbo / Tom's Hardware May 27 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung's largest union ratified the tentative agreement. 62,616 of 65,593 eligible members voted (95.5%). 46,142 YES votes (73.7%). SGUU (DS-heavy) 80.6% approval. NSEU (DX-heavy) 21.1% approval. DS employees get 10.5% operating profit in stock bonuses over 10 years (~$340K-$540K per person). DX employees get flat 6M won (~$4,000). Strike risk formally eliminated. BUT new operational risk emerged: bonus resentment causing packaging division slowdowns.
